pattern: The most square-like factor pair is found by scanning divisors down from the square root.
hints: The width can never exceed the square root of the area.
Start the candidate width at the floor of the square root and step downward until it divides the area.
The first divisor found going down gives the smallest length-minus-width gap; return length then width.

edge_inputs.description: prime area forces a thin rectangle
perfect square of a larger number
near the maximum, a large prime

stress.description: random areas across the full range
